tiparivaṭṭaṁ.meaning: the three rounds; threefold turning (of the Dhamma wheel)

tipiṭaka.meaning: the Tipiṭaka, the “three baskets” — the threefold collection of the Pāli Canon
breakdown: Basket, collection — the Vinaya, Sutta and Abhidhamma collections.
And (the preceding ṁ becomes ñ before c).
Root (desiderative formation): to endure, to bear patiently.

tittakalābubījaṁ.meaning: the seed of the bitter gourd

tittakattāya.meaning: to bitterness; for the state of being bitter

titthakaro.meaning: a ford-maker; a religious founder, founder of a sect
breakdown: Ford, crossing place; figuratively a religious system.

titthiyasamādānaṁ.meaning: undergoing what the Titthiyas (sectarian ascetics) do; taking up the observances of other sects
breakdown: A sectarian ascetic, an adherent of another school.
titthiyasamādānaṁ.goenka: The Titthiya ascetics are mentioned in the Mahāvagga (Uposathakkhandhaka): King Bimbisāra, while in meditation, notices that the Titthiyas hold regular meetings where their followers visit them and ask for Dhamma to be preached. He suggests to the Buddha that the Bhikkhus should do likewise for their own benefit and the benefit of their lay-followers, and the Buddha agrees, inviting the Bhikkhus to assemble in the same way.

tiṇagge.meaning: on the tip of a blade of grass

tiṇukkūpamā.meaning: the simile of the grass torch
breakdown: Torch (tiṇukkā: a torch made of grass; the final ā merges with the following u).

tiṇṇa.meaning: crossed over; one who has overcome

tiṇṇaṁ.gram: cardinal numeral, genitive plural of ti

tiṭṭhaṁ.gram: present participle of tiṭṭhati (√ṭhā), masculine nominative singular
breakdown: Nominative singular of the present participle (tiṭṭhanta).

tulādhāro.meaning: one holding the scales; a weigher

tulākūṭakaṁsakūṭamānakūṭā.meaning: false scales, false plates (coinage) and false measures — deception in weighing, coinage and measuring

tusitapure.meaning: in the Tusita city; in the celestial city of the Tusita devas
breakdown: Tusita, the heaven of the contented devas.

tuṇhībhūtaṁ.gram: past participle of tuṇhībhavati, accusative singular

tuṇhībhūtā.gram: past participle of tuṇhībhavati, nominative plural / feminine singular

tveva.meaning: thus indeed; in this very way
breakdown: Quotation marker iti, contracted (or the particle tu).

tāsu.gram: pronoun, locative feminine plural of ta

tāsveva.meaning: in those very ones; just in those
tāsveva.gram: sandhi fusion (tāsu + eva), pronoun locative feminine plural + emphatic particle
breakdown: In those (locative feminine plural; a connecting v bridges to eva).
Emphatic particle: “even, just” — emphasis, as in the repetition tāsu tāsu.

tāta.meaning: dear one; respected one; father (also used affectionately for a son)

tāva.meaning: so much; so long; first of all

tāvatiṁsā.meaning: the Tāvatiṁsa devas; the realm of the Thirty-three gods
tāvatiṁsā.summary: The heaven of the Thirty-three, considered to stand on top of the mythical mountain Sineru (Meru).
breakdown: Thirty-three (te-ttiṁsa): the heaven of the thirty-three gods.

tīragocarā.meaning: dwelling along the bank; having the shore as their feeding ground

tīramevānudhāvati.meaning: runs along just this shore; follows along the very bank
tīramevānudhāvati.gram: sandhi fusion (tīraṁ + eva + anudhāvati), verb phrase
breakdown: Just, this very (emphatic; merges with the following a: evānu-).

tīsu sikkhāsu.meaning: in the three trainings (sīla, samādhi, paññā)
breakdown: In the trainings (locative plural of sikkhā).
tīsu sikkhāsu.goenka: The three trainings — tīsu sikkhāsu — are sīla (morality), samādhi (concentration, mastery of the mind) and paññā (experiential wisdom). They form the framework of the entire path: moral conduct is the base, concentration steadies the mind upon it, and wisdom purifies it through direct insight.

tīṇiyeva.gram: sandhi fusion (tīṇi + [y] + eva), numeral + emphatic particle
breakdown: Emphatic particle: “just, only” (a connecting y bridges the two words).

ubbalakhādikā: d.meaning: eating away one's strength; consumption of one's vigour (dubbalakhādikā)
breakdown: Prefix: bad, poor (the initial d is detached by a line break in the source: dubbala).
Strength (with sandhi doubling: dubbala “weak, of poor strength”).
ubbalakhādikā: d.note: uncertain: the source lemma is split by a line break; the full word is dubbalakhādikā “eating away the strength (of the weak)”.

ubhayabyābādhāyapi.meaning: also to the affliction of both (sides)

ubhayattha.meaning: in both places; in both cases

breakdown: Root meaning “to cut”; uccheda is the cutting-off, annihilation.

ucchijjati.meaning: ceases; is annihilated, gets cut off
ucchijjati.gram: verb, 3rd person singular, passive of ucchindati (√chid)

ucchādanaparimaddananhāpanasamb.meaning: anointing, massaging and bathing (first part of ucchādanaparimaddananhāpanasambāhanaṁ)
ucchādanaparimaddananhāpanasamb.note: uncertain: this is a truncated fragment of ucchādanaparimaddananhāpanasambāhanaṁ in the source, not an independent word.

ucchādanaparimaddananhāpanasambāhanaṁ.meaning: anointing with perfumes and oil, rubbing, bathing and massage

ucchādanaparimaddananhāpana­.meaning: anointing, rubbing and bathing (first part of ucchādanaparimaddananhāpanasambāhanaṁ)
ucchādanaparimaddananhāpana­.note: uncertain: this is a hyphenated line-break fragment of ucchādanaparimaddananhāpanasambāhanaṁ in the source, not an independent word.

uccini.gram: verb, aorist 3rd person singular of uccināti (ud + √ci)
breakdown: Root meaning “to gather, collect”: uccināti “to pick out, select”.

uccāsayana high.meaning: a high bed; lofty couch (duplicate listing of uccāsayana)
uccāsayana high.note: uncertain: the key includes the stray English word “high” from the source layout; the Pāli lemma is uccāsayana.

udakalekhūpamo.meaning: like writing on water; comparable to a line drawn on water
breakdown: Writing, line, scratch (final vowel merges with the following u).
